title: Dynamic Programming: Edit Distance (Java / Python)
general_notes: No double deduction (except in point 5). Do not deduct again for an error already penalised in an earlier point. For example: if the base case is wrong, base the recurrence marks on whether the student applies the recurrence correctly relative to their own initialisation.
This rubric applies to both Java and Python submissions. The Java method is editDistance(String a, String b); the Python function is editDistance(a, b). Both are correct.
Recursive implementations must be penalised in point 5: the task explicitly requires an iterative (non-recursive) solution.
Edit distance is defined as the minimum number of single-character insert, delete, and replace operations to transform string a into string b.

rubric_points.name: DP table allocation and loop structure
rubric_points.target: A 2D table of size (m+1) x (n+1) is allocated, and nested loops iterate i from 1 to m and j from 1 to n to fill it bottom-up.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: No 2D table is allocated, or only a 1D array is used; no nested loops are present to fill the table.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: A 2D table and nested loops are present, but the table size is wrong (e.g., m x n instead of (m+1) x (n+1)), or the loop bounds are off (e.g., starting from 0 instead of 1, or not reaching m or n).

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: A 2D table of size (m+1) x (n+1) is allocated and nested loops correctly iterate i from 1 to m (inclusive) and j from 1 to n (inclusive) to fill it bottom-up.

rubric_points.name: Base case initialisation
rubric_points.target: edDis[i][0] = i for all i from 0 to m (cost of deleting i characters), and edDis[0][j] = j for all j from 0 to n (cost of inserting j characters).

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: Neither base case is initialised; the first row and column remain 0, making the DP table incorrect for any non-empty string.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: Only one of the two base cases is correctly initialised (first row or first column), while the other is missing or set to a wrong value.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: Both base cases are correctly initialised: edDis[i][0] = i for all i from 0 to m, and edDis[0][j] = j for all j from 0 to n.

rubric_points.name: Character match case
rubric_points.target: When a[i-1] == b[j-1], edDis[i][j] is set to edDis[i-1][j-1] (no edit needed).

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: No character comparison is performed, or the match case is absent and the mismatch formula is applied unconditionally.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: A character comparison is present but uses wrong indices (e.g., a[i] == b[j] instead of a[i-1] == b[j-1]), or on a match the wrong cell is copied (e.g., copies edDis[i][j-1] instead of edDis[i-1][j-1]).

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: When a[i-1] == b[j-1], edDis[i][j] is correctly set to edDis[i-1][j-1].

rubric_points.name: Mismatch recurrence: 1 + min of three neighbours
rubric_points.target: When a[i-1] != b[j-1], edDis[i][j] = 1 + min(edDis[i][j-1], edDis[i-1][j], edDis[i-1][j-1]), representing insert, delete, and replace respectively.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: No recurrence is applied for the mismatch case, or a constant value is used instead of referencing the table.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: A min-based recurrence is present but incomplete or incorrect: for example, only two of the three neighbours are taken, the +1 cost is missing, or the wrong cells are referenced.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: edDis[i][j] = 1 + min(edDis[i][j-1], edDis[i-1][j], edDis[i-1][j-1]) is applied correctly when characters differ, covering all three edit operations.

rubric_points.name: Overall correctness
rubric_points.target: The method returns the correct edit distance for all valid string inputs without using recursion.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: Method returns 0 or an incorrect value for most inputs, or uses recursion (which is explicitly forbidden).

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: Returns correct results for simple cases (e.g., empty string, identical strings, single character) but fails for longer or more complex inputs.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: Returns the correct edit distance for all valid string inputs using an iterative (non-recursive) implementation.

buckets.label: Internally consistent but incorrect final result
buckets.description: Logic follows consistently from the student's own table, base cases, and recurrence, but the final result is incorrect due to a systematic error (e.g., consistently wrong index offset or missing +1 in the recurrence).
